zh.Great Melton, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可格亞特 梅爾敦
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys於1186年出生在英國諾福爾可格亞特 梅爾敦。她於1207年與Henry Teyes在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Tyeys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Camille, Denys, Emile, Philippe.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Theijs, from a short form of the personal name Mathijs, Dutch form of Matthew .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys was born in 1186, in Great Melton, Norfolk, England. She married Henry Teyes in 1207, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys est née en 1186, à Great Melton, Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Henry Teyes en 1207, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys wurde 1186 in Great Melton, Norfolk, England geboren. Sie hat 1207 in England, United Kingdom Henry Teyes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys è nata il nel 1186 nel Great Melton, Norfolk, England. Lei sposato Henry Teyes nel 1207, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Great Melton, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクグレアト メルトン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeysは1186年にイギリスノーフォルクグレアト メルトンで生まれた。1207年にイギリスイギリスでHenry Teyesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys(은)는 1186년 영국 노르폴크 그리트 멜턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1207년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Henry Teyes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys nasceu em 1186, em Great Melton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Henry Teyes em 1207, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys родилась в 1186 г. в местности грит мелтон, норфолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Teyes в 1207 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Isabel Tyeys nació en 1186, en Great Melton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Henry Teyes en 1207, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
